indication: Second line treatment of hormone resistant prostate cancer following chemotherapy (NICE); First line treatment of hormone resistant prostate cancer where chemotherapy is contra-indicated (CDF); Performance status 0, 1, 2

chemotherapy_drugs.special_instructions: Take on an empty stomach at least two hours after food or at least one hour before food. Swallow tablets whole with water. Available as 250mg tablets.
Take with or after food. Dose may need to be reduced gradually before stopping treatment. Available as 5mg (uncoated) and 2.5mg and 5mg (enteric coated) tablets.

take_home_medicines.instructions: Take on an empty stomach at least two hours after food or at least one hour before food.
Take with or after food. The dose of this medicine may need to be reduced gradually before stopping treatment.

dose_modifications.notes: Withhold treatment. May restart at 500mg daily once LFTs return to baseline.
Stop treatment permanently.
Stop treatment permanently.

dose_modifications.condition: Pre-existing moderate or severe hepatic impairment (Child-Pugh Class B or C)
dose_modifications.notes: Abiraterone should be avoided; no safety data to support use.
Use with caution in severe renal impairment.

treatment_delay_criteria.notes: Abiraterone is not myelosuppressive; haematological dose modifications not applicable. Consider blood transfusion if Hb <8g/dL.

monitoring_requirements: U&Es
Liver function (serum transaminases and bilirubin) prior to starting treatment, every two weeks for first three months, then monthly thereafter
Blood pressure, serum potassium and fluid retention before treatment and at least monthly thereafter
Cholesterol and triglycerides every four months
special_warnings: Patients should continue to receive an LHRH agonist during abiraterone treatment
Use with caution in patients with history of cardiovascular disease; safety not established in patients with LVEF <50% or NYHA Class III or IV heart failure
Before treatment, hypertension must be controlled and hypokalaemia must be corrected
Patients who stop abiraterone may require gradual withdrawal of prednisolone
Gastric protection with PPI or H2 antagonist may be considered in patients at high risk of GI ulceration or bleed
Abiraterone treatment should be supervised by a consultant oncologist

drugs.special_instructions: Take on an empty stomach at least two hours after food or at least one hour before food. Swallow tablets whole with water. Available as 250mg tablets.
drugs.notes: once daily | Take on an empty stomach at least two hours after food or at least one hour before food. Swallow tablets whole with water. Available as 250mg tablets.

drugs.special_instructions: Take with or after food. Dose may need to be reduced gradually before stopping treatment. Available as 5mg (uncoated) and 2.5mg and 5mg (enteric coated) tablets.
drugs.notes: twice daily | Take with or after food. Dose may need to be reduced gradually before stopping treatment. Available as 5mg (uncoated) and 2.5mg and 5mg (enteric coated) tablets.

indication: Non-metastatic castrate-resistant prostate cancer (nmCRPC) with PSA doubling time <=10 months and PSA >=2ng/ml, in combination with ADT; OR newly diagnosed metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer (mHSPC) in combination with ADT, in patients ineligible for or declining docetaxel. WHO performance status 0, 1, or 2.

chemotherapy_drugs.special_instructions: Swallow whole, with or without food. Available as 60mg film-coated tablets (4 tablets = 240mg). If dose missed, take as soon as possible on same day; do not double dose.

take_home_medicines.instructions: Swallow whole, either with or without food.

dose_modifications.notes: Continue apalutamide at current dose. Initiate topical steroid cream AND oral antihistamine. Monitor for change in severity.
Hold apalutamide for up to 28 days. Initiate topical steroid cream AND oral antihistamine. When rash improves to Grade <=1, reinitiate; consider dose reduction to 180mg daily (dose level -1).

Hold apalutamide for up to 28 days. Topical steroid cream AND oral antihistamine. Consider short course oral steroid. Reassess after 2 weeks; if unchanged or worse, initiate oral steroids and refer to dermatology. Reinitiate at dose level -1 (180mg) when rash resolved to Grade <=1. If dose reduction leads to <120mg, discontinue.

dose_modifications.condition: Grade 3 or greater toxicity or intolerable adverse reaction
dose_modifications.notes: Withhold until symptoms improve to Grade 1 or below. Resume at same dose or reduced dose (180mg or 120mg) if warranted. Dose level -1: 180mg daily; Dose level -2: 120mg daily.
Not recommended in severe hepatic impairment; no data available.
Caution required; not studied in severe renal impairment. Monitor for adverse reactions and dose reduce accordingly.
Monitor thyroid function regularly. Initiate or dose-adjust thyroid replacement therapy as indicated. Do not necessarily stop apalutamide.

treatment_delay_criteria.notes: No specific haematological thresholds defined for apalutamide. FBC, U&Es, LFTs, PSA and BP every 4 weeks for first 12 weeks, then every 8-12 weeks thereafter.

monitoring_requirements: FBC, U&Es, LFTs, PSA, thyroid function tests and blood pressure at baseline
FBC, U&Es, LFTs, PSA and blood pressure every four weeks for first 12 weeks then every 8-12 weeks thereafter
Thyroid function tests if clinically indicated
special_warnings: Apalutamide is a strong inducer of CYP3A4 and CYP2C19 and a weak inducer of CYP2C9 - significant drug interactions; review concomitant medications
Co-administration with warfarin and coumarin-like anticoagulants should be avoided; if unavoidable, additional INR monitoring required
Consider Calcium + Vitamin D supplements (Adcal D3 2 tablets daily) for bone health; DEXA scan for moderate/high risk FRAX score
Formal medical review at least by start of 3rd 4-weekly cycle
Apalutamide treatment should be supervised by a consultant oncologist
Rash reported in 25% of patients; hypothyroidism reported in 30% of patients on thyroid replacement and 7% not on replacement
Uncommon but serious risk of seizure

drugs.special_instructions: Swallow whole, with or without food. Available as 60mg film-coated tablets (4 tablets = 240mg). If dose missed, take as soon as possible on same day; do not double dose.
drugs.notes: once daily | Swallow whole, with or without food. Available as 60mg film-coated tablets (4 tablets = 240mg). If dose missed, take as soon as possible on same day; do not double dose.

indication: First line treatment of locally advanced or metastatic urothelial cancer in patients ineligible for cisplatin-based chemotherapy whose tumours have PD-L1 expression >=5% (ATE1); OR second line treatment of locally advanced or metastatic urothelial cancer previously treated with platinum-based chemotherapy, up to maximum 2 years / 35 administrations (ATE3). Blueteq application required.

chemotherapy_drugs.special_instructions: Administer 15mL subcutaneously into the thigh over approximately 7 minutes. Alternate between left and right thigh only. New injections at least 2.5cm from old site, never into red, bruised, tender or hard skin. Use SC infusion set (winged/butterfly). DO NOT administer residual hold-up volume in tubing. Patient must be given atezolizumab Patient Alert Card.

when required for treatment of injection-related reactions
when required for relief of injection-related reactions

dose_modifications.notes: No dose reductions permitted for atezolizumab. Only delay or discontinue. Funding approval precludes further treatment after interruption of 12 weeks or longer.

dose_modifications.condition: Bilirubin 1.5-3xULN OR AST/ALT 3-5xULN (Grade 2 hepatitis persisting 5-7 days)
dose_modifications.notes: Withhold atezolizumab. Consider corticosteroid; taper over at least 1 month if LFTs improve. Resume when event improves to Grade 1 within 12 weeks and corticosteroid reduced to <=10mg prednisolone/day equivalent.
Permanently discontinue atezolizumab.

Withhold until symptoms resolve and radiographic abnormalities improve. Consider oral prednisolone 1-2mg/kg/day. Resume if improves to Grade 0-1 within 12 weeks and corticosteroids reduced to <=10mg prednisone/day.
Permanently discontinue atezolizumab. Consider corticosteroids.

dose_modifications.condition: Grade 2 or 3 diarrhoea or symptomatic colitis
dose_modifications.notes: Withhold initially. Grade 2: if persistent >5 days or recurs, start prednisolone 1-2mg/kg/day oral. Grade 3: IV corticosteroids, convert to oral as symptoms improve, taper over 1 month. Resume if improves to Grade 0-1 within 12 weeks and corticosteroids <=10mg/day.
Permanently discontinue atezolizumab.
Withhold. Resume if amylase/lipase improve to Grade 0-1 within 12 weeks and pancreatitis resolved and corticosteroids <=10mg/day.

dose_modifications.condition: Grade 4 or any grade recurrent pancreatitis
dose_modifications.notes: Permanently discontinue atezolizumab.
Withhold. Hypothyroidism: resume when controlled by thyroid replacement and TSH decreasing. Hyperthyroidism: resume when controlled by carbimazole or equivalent and thyroid function improving.
Withhold. Resume if improves to Grade 0-1 within 12 weeks, corticosteroids <=10mg/day and patient stable on replacement therapy.
Withhold. Start prednisolone 1-2mg/kg/day. Resume if improves to Grade 0-1 within 12 weeks and corticosteroids <=10mg/day.
Permanently discontinue atezolizumab.
Withhold. Resume when improves to Grade 0-1 within 12 weeks, corticosteroids <=10mg/day and stable on replacement therapy.
Permanently discontinue atezolizumab.
Withhold. Resume if metabolic control achieved on insulin replacement therapy.
Permanently discontinue atezolizumab.
Withhold atezolizumab.
Permanently discontinue atezolizumab.
Reduce injection rate or pause injection. Resume when event resolved.
Permanently discontinue atezolizumab.
Withhold. Resume when improves to Grade 0-1 within 12 weeks and corticosteroids <=10mg/day.
Permanently discontinue atezolizumab.

Blueteq application required before commencing treatment (ATE1 or ATE3)

indication: Treatment of adult patients with locally advanced or metastatic urothelial carcinoma after disease progression following one prior platinum-containing regimen regardless of setting (neoadjuvant, adjuvant, metastatic). WHO performance status 0, 1.

indication: COVID-19 pandemic protocol for inoperable locally advanced or metastatic urothelial cancer (T4b any N or any T N2-3, or any T any N M1) who would normally receive 1st line platinum-based chemotherapy, no prior chemotherapy for metastatic/locally advanced disease, PD-L1 tested (clinicians should be aware of decreased survival signal in PD-L1 <5% subgroup), WHO PS 0-2. Maximum treatment duration 2 years (26 cycles at 4-weekly schedule).

indication: First-line maintenance treatment of adult patients with locally advanced or metastatic urothelial carcinoma (UC) whose disease has not progressed with first-line treatment with gemcitabine plus cisplatin or carboplatin (at least 4 and no more than 6 cycles). Patient must commence avelumab within 4-10 weeks of last chemotherapy dose. WHO Performance status 0, 1.

chemotherapy_drugs.special_instructions: Administer via sterile, non-pyrogenic 0.2 micron in-line filter. Patients must be given an avelumab alert card.

pre_medications.timing: 30 minutes prior to avelumab (cycles 1-4; may omit from cycle 5 onwards if no infusion-related reactions)
30 minutes prior to avelumab (cycles 1-4; may omit from cycle 5 onwards if no infusion-related reactions)

dose_modifications.notes: No dose reductions for avelumab. Treatment delayed rather than reduced. If third dose reduction is necessary, stop treatment.
Treatment threshold: neutrophils >=1x10^9/L required. Delay treatment.
Treatment threshold: platelets >=100x10^9/L required. Delay treatment.
Reduce infusion rate by 50%.
Withhold until adverse reactions recover to Grade 0-1; restart infusion with 50% slower rate.
Permanently discontinue avelumab.

Permanently discontinue avelumab.

dose_modifications.notes: Withhold until adverse reactions recover to Grade 0-1. Hypothyroidism managed by standard medical practice; no need to discontinue avelumab.

dose_modifications.condition: Serum creatinine >1.5 and up to 6x ULN
dose_modifications.notes: Withhold until adverse reactions recover to Grade 0-1.
Permanently discontinue avelumab.
Withhold until recover to Grade 0-1. If persistent >5 days, consider corticosteroid taper. Resume when Grade 1 following corticosteroid taper.

dose_modifications.condition: ALT/AST >=5xULN or >3xULN with concurrent bilirubin >=2xULN or bilirubin >=3xULN
dose_modifications.notes: Permanently discontinue avelumab. Consider corticosteroids.
Withhold. Administer corticosteroids. On improvement to Grade 1 begin to taper over 1 month. Restart when colitis at Grade 1 following taper.

dose_modifications.condition: NCI-CTC Grade 4, or not improved to Grade 1 within 12 weeks, or corticosteroids cannot be reduced to <=10mg/day within 12 weeks
dose_modifications.notes: Permanently discontinue avelumab.

dose_modifications.condition: Grade 2 or 3 immune-related adverse reaction not described above
dose_modifications.notes: Withhold until adverse reactions recover to Grade 0-1.

dose_modifications.condition: Life-threatening/Grade 4; recurrent Grade 3; >=10mg prednisone/day for >12 weeks; persistent Grade 2-3 lasting >=12 weeks
dose_modifications.notes: Permanently discontinue avelumab.

treatment_delay_criteria.notes: Avelumab dose not generally reduced for haematological toxicity; delayed rather than reduced.

monitoring_requirements: FBC, LFTs and U&Es prior to day 1 for six cycles then, if stable, every four weeks thereafter
Thyroid function tests prior to starting treatment then every four weeks for twelve weeks (at least every 12 weeks thereafter) or when clinically indicated
Ensure adequate cardiac function before starting; baseline LVEF in patients with cardiac history or elderly; repeat every 3-6 months as indicated
special_warnings: No dose reductions are permitted for avelumab; treatment delayed rather than reduced
Immune-related adverse reactions can be severe or life-threatening
Pre-medication (chlorphenamine 10mg IV and paracetamol 1000mg oral) required 30 minutes before infusions 1-4; may be omitted from cycle 5 onwards if no infusion-related reactions have occurred
9% incidence of NCI-CTC Grade 3/4 raised transaminases and 6.3% immune-related hepatitis with this regimen
Patients must be given an avelumab alert card to carry at all times
Treatment break of >12 weeks beyond expected cycle length requires treatment break form to restart
Formal medical review at least by end of first 8 weeks of treatment

indication: Unresectable locally advanced or metastatic renal cell carcinoma (RCC) with clear cell component or papillary/chromophobe/collecting duct/medullary/mucinous tubular and spindle cell/multilocular cystic/XP11 translocation RCC. Patient must be treatment naive or prior systemic therapy completed >=12 months previously (adjuvant/neoadjuvant context). No symptomatic brain or leptomeningeal metastases. WHO performance status 0, 1, 2.

chemotherapy_drugs.special_instructions: Administer via sterile, non-pyrogenic 0.2 micron in-line filter. Avelumab dose is delayed rather than reduced. Patients must be given an avelumab alert card.

Starting dose 5mg twice daily. Dose escalation to 7mg BD (days 15-28 cycle 1 if tolerated) then 10mg BD (cycle 2 onwards if tolerated) - see dose escalation protocol. Swallow whole approximately 12 hours apart. Do not chew or crush. Available as 1mg, 3mg, 5mg and 7mg tablets. Dose reductions: 5mg BD to 3mg BD to 2mg BD.

pre_medications.timing: 30 minutes prior to avelumab (cycles 1-4; may omit from cycle 5 onwards if no infusion-related reactions)
30 minutes prior to avelumab (cycles 1-4; may omit from cycle 5 onwards if no infusion-related reactions)

take_home_medicines.instructions: Swallow whole approximately 12 hours apart. Do not chew or crush. Dose escalation may be considered per protocol.

dose_modifications.condition: Tolerates 5mg BD with no adverse effects >NCI-CTC Grade 2 for two consecutive weeks AND BP <=150/90 AND not on antihypertensives
dose_modifications.notes: Cycle 1 days 1-14: 5mg BD. Cycle 1 days 15-28: may escalate to 7mg BD if above criteria met. Cycle 2 onwards: may escalate to 10mg BD if tolerated 7mg BD by same criteria. In practice dose escalation rarely tolerated. ARIA will be set at 5mg BD.
Dose reductions in incremental steps: 5mg BD to 3mg BD to 2mg BD. If third dose reduction necessary, stop treatment.
Cycle 1 requirement: neutrophils >=1x10^9/L. Thereafter: delay until recovery to >=1x10^9/L. If recovery within 7 days, continue at last dose. If >7 days, reduce dose.
Cycle 1 requirement: platelets >=75x10^9/L. Thereafter: delay until recovery to >=75x10^9/L. If within 7 days, continue at full dose; if >7 days, reduce dose.
Axitinib starting dose 2mg twice daily for Child-Pugh B. Child-Pugh C: no information.
No information available for axitinib in CrCl <=15ml/min.

dose_modifications.condition: Persistent BP >140/90mmHg despite standard antihypertensive therapy
dose_modifications.notes: Reduce axitinib dose and continue to monitor. If hypertension persists, discontinue axitinib.

Stop both avelumab and axitinib. Treatment may be restarted with dose reduction of one dose level of axitinib in the first instance. Grade 2-3 colitis: withhold both, administer corticosteroids. Permanently discontinue avelumab if colitis does not improve to Grade 1 within 12 weeks.
Withhold both until Grade 0-1. If persistent >5 days, consider corticosteroids with taper. After NCI-CTC Grade 2 hepatitis recovery, consider sequential rechallenge with axitinib with dose reduction.

dose_modifications.condition: ALT/AST >=5xULN or >3xULN with bilirubin >=2xULN or bilirubin >=3xULN
dose_modifications.notes: Permanently discontinue both avelumab and axitinib. Consider corticosteroids.
Delay axitinib until resolves to Grade 1. Restart with dose reduction.
Delay axitinib until resolves to Grade 1. Restart with reduced dose.

dose_modifications.condition: Moderate to severe proteinuria (>=2+ on dipstick or >1g/24h)
dose_modifications.notes: Reduce axitinib dose or temporarily interrupt. Discontinue if nephrotic syndrome develops.
Reduce infusion rate by 50%.
Withhold until adverse reactions recover to Grade 0-1; restart with 50% slower rate.
Permanently discontinue avelumab.

indication: Second line treatment of advanced or metastatic renal cell carcinoma after failure or intolerance to a first line therapy such as sunitinib. WHO Performance status 0, 1. Palliative intent.

chemotherapy_drugs.special_instructions: Starting dose 5mg twice daily (days 1-14 cycle 1). Escalate to 7mg BD (days 15-28 cycle 1) if tolerated with no adverse effects >Grade 2 for 2 consecutive weeks AND BP <=150/90 AND not on antihypertensives. Escalate to 10mg BD (cycle 2 onwards) if 7mg BD tolerated. Dose reductions: 5mg BD to 3mg BD to 2mg BD. Swallow whole; do not chew or crush. Take approximately 12 hours apart. Available as 1mg, 3mg, 5mg and 7mg tablets.

take_home_medicines.instructions: Swallow whole. Do not chew or crush. Dose escalation per protocol.

dose_modifications.condition: Tolerates 5mg BD with no adverse effects >NCI-CTC Grade 2 for two consecutive weeks AND BP <=150/90 AND not on antihypertensives
dose_modifications.notes: Cycle 1: 5mg BD days 1-14, increase to 7mg BD days 15-28 if tolerated. Cycle 2 onwards: increase to 10mg BD if tolerated 7mg BD by same criteria. ARIA note: dose has been automatically escalated; verify appropriateness.
Cycle 1 requirement: >=1x10^9/L. Thereafter delay until recovery. If within 7 days, continue at last dose; if >7 days, reduce dose.
Cycle 1 requirement: >=75x10^9/L. Thereafter delay until recovery. If within 7 days, continue at full dose; if >7 days, reduce dose.
Starting dose 2mg twice daily for Child-Pugh B. Child-Pugh C: no information.
No information available.

dose_modifications.condition: Persistent BP >140/90mmHg despite standard antihypertensive therapy
dose_modifications.notes: Reduce axitinib dose and continue to monitor. If hypertension persists, discontinue axitinib.
Reduce dose by one dose level.
Stop axitinib until resolves to at least Grade 2. Restart with dose reduction of one dose level.
Delay axitinib until resolves to Grade 1. Restart with dose reduction.
Delay axitinib until resolves to Grade 1. Restart with reduced dose.
Reduce axitinib dose or temporarily interrupt. Discontinue if nephrotic syndrome develops.

treatment_delay_criteria.notes: Cycle 1: neutrophils >=1x10^9/L and platelets >=75x10^9/L required. If third dose reduction necessary, stop treatment.

monitoring_requirements: FBC, LFTs and U&Es prior to each cycle for first three cycles; may reduce to every other cycle if stable
Blood pressure weekly for first 4 weeks then every 6-12 weeks
Thyroid function tests at baseline then every 3 months
Baseline LVEF in patients with cardiac history or elderly; repeat every 3-6 months as clinically indicated
special_warnings: Axitinib dose escalation protocol: 5mg BD (cycle 1 days 1-14) to 7mg BD (cycle 1 days 15-28 if tolerated) to 10mg BD (cycle 2 onwards if tolerated)
Dose reductions in incremental steps: 5mg BD to 3mg BD to 2mg BD; stop if third reduction necessary
Axitinib should be stopped at least 24 hours prior to scheduled surgery
Hypothyroidism can occur; manage according to standard practice, no need to dose reduce axitinib
Palmar-plantar erythrodysaesthesia: apply moisturiser regularly; minimise pressure on hands/feet; chiropodist referral if appropriate
Axitinib is associated with drug interactions
Must be prescribed under supervision of an oncologist
National Patient Safety Alert on oral SACT (NPSA/2008/RRR001) applies
This regimen may require funding approval

required_patient_fields.prior_first_line_therapy: failure or intolerance to sunitinib or other 1st line therapy

drugs.special_instructions: Starting dose 5mg twice daily (days 1-14 cycle 1). Escalate to 7mg BD (days 15-28 cycle 1) if tolerated with no adverse effects >Grade 2 for 2 consecutive weeks AND BP <=150/90 AND not on antihypertensives. Escalate to 10mg BD (cycle 2 onwards) if 7mg BD tolerated. Dose reductions: 5mg BD to 3mg BD to 2mg BD. Swallow whole; do not chew or crush. Take approximately 12 hours apart. Available as 1mg, 3mg, 5mg and 7mg tablets.
drugs.notes: twice daily | Starting dose 5mg twice daily (days 1-14 cycle 1). Escalate to 7mg BD (days 15-28 cycle 1) if tolerated with no adverse effects >Grade 2 for 2 consecutive weeks AND BP <=150/90 AND not on antihypertensives. Escalate to 10mg BD (cycle 2 onwards) if 7mg BD tolerated. Dose reductions: 5mg BD to 3mg BD to 2mg BD. Swallow whole; do not chew or crush. Take approximately 12 hours apart. Available as 1mg, 3mg, 5mg and 7mg tablets.
